name: Bean Soup from Tuscany
recipeIngredient: 3 Fresh Tomatoes OR 3 Canned Italian Tomatoes
1/2 Teaspoon Dried Thyme OR 1/2 Teaspoon Tarragon
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans overnight in cold water; rinse and drain.
2. Coarsely chop one onion, parsley, celery, garlic, basil, and rosemary. Place in a stockpot.
3. Add beans, coarse-grained salt to taste, and 5 quarts of cold water. Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat, then simmer for about 1 1/2 hours.
4. Finely chop the remaining onion, chop up the tomatoes in a food processor, and cut the ham and salt pork into small pieces.
5. Heat 1/4 cup of olive oil and lightly saute the onion, ham, and salt pork for about 10 minutes. Add the tomatoes. Taste for salt and pepper and add the thyme or tarragon. Simmer for about 10 minutes.
6. Clean and wash the cabbage. Cut into 1/2-inch strips. When the beans have finished cooking, remove about half the beans and vegetables with a slotted spoon and puree in a food processor. Return the puree to the stockpot and add enough cold water to make about 4 quarts liquid. Bring to a boil.
7. Add cabbage and simmer for about 25 minutes, then add tomatoes and meat to the stockpot and simmer 5 minutes longer.
8. Toast the slices of bread and place in each individual soup bowl. Ladle the soup over the bread and serve, adding a teaspoon of olive oil to each serving.
